description:
You are assisting a researcher who is tidying-up the Python package list that belongs to a large collection of datasets.

    Pre-existing material:
      • A directory /home/user/data/experiment/ already exists.
      • Inside that directory there is a text file called requirements_raw.txt which contains the following **exact five lines** (new-line terminated, in this exact order):

            numpy==1.23.5
            pandas==1.5.3
            scipy==1.9.3
            numpy==1.23.5
            matplotlib==3.6.2

    Goal:
      1. Produce a **deduplicated, alphabetically-sorted** requirements file named
         /home/user/data/experiment/requirements_clean.txt

         – Each logical entry must appear **once and only once**.  
         – The sort order must be ordinary ASCII/lexicographical order (e.g. “matplotlib…” then “numpy…”, etc.).  
         – Every line must end with a single Unix new-line character (`\n`).  
         – No blank lines are allowed anywhere in the file (including at the end).  
         – The finished file must therefore contain **exactly four lines** that look like this:

              matplotlib==3.6.2
              numpy==1.23.5
              pandas==1.5.3
              scipy==1.9.3

      2. Accomplish the entire transformation with **one single compound shell command** (pipelines are allowed, but do not break the command into multiple separate interactive steps).

    What the automated checker will verify:
      • The file /home/user/data/experiment/requirements_clean.txt exists.  
      • It contains exactly four non-empty lines.  
      • Those lines match the four package specifications shown above **in that exact order** with no extra whitespace or blank lines.  

    You do NOT need to remove the original requirements_raw.txt file.  
    Once the correct requirements_clean.txt file is present and formatted exactly as specified, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial state (already present when the session starts):
        File: /home/user/data/experiment/requirements_raw.txt
        Content (with a final newline after each line):

            numpy==1.23.5\n
            pandas==1.5.3\n
            scipy==1.9.3\n
            numpy==1.23.5\n
            matplotlib==3.6.2\n

    Expected state after successful task completion:
        File: /home/user/data/experiment/requirements_clean.txt
        Permissions: default user-writable file (rw-r--r--)
        Content (each line followed by a single trailing \n and **nothing else**):

            matplotlib==3.6.2\n
            numpy==1.23.5\n
            pandas==1.5.3\n
            scipy==1.9.3\n
